seoTitle: Mount Burnett vs Mount Camel — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Burnett and Mount Camel. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Burnett vs Mount Camel: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Burnett and Mount Camel.
heading: Mount Burnett vs Mount Camel — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Burnett, Victoria and Mount Camel, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Camel has a lower overall climate risk score (26/100) compared to Mount Burnett (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Burnett or Mount Camel safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Burnett has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Mount Camel scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Burnett scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Camel scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Burnett or Mount Camel?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Camel has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.62% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
